name: list_autonomous_systems
description: List Autonomous Systems

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

name: get_as_details
description: Get Autonomous System details by ASN

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

name: get_ip_details
description: Get IP address information including full ASN details (name, country, population estimates from APNIC).

ip.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address in standard notation.

name: get_traffic_anomalies
description: Get traffic anomalies and outages

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

name: get_internet_services_ranking
description: Get top Internet services

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

serviceCategory.description: Filters results by Internet service category.

name: get_domains_ranking
description: Get top or trending domains

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a comma-separated list of alpha-2 location codes.

name: get_domain_rank_details
description: Get domain rank details

domain.description: A valid domain name, e.g., example.com or sub.domain.co.uk. Must follow standard domain formatting rules without protocol or path.

name: get_http_data
description: Retrieve HTTP traffic trends.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

geoId.description: Filters results by Geolocation (ADM1 - administrative level 1, e.g., states/provinces). Provide an array of GeoNames IDs. Prefix with '-' to exclude.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of HTTP data to retrieve.

normalization.description: Normalization method applied to results. timeseries accepts PERCENTAGE_CHANGE or MIN0_MAX; timeseries_groups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX.

name: get_dns_queries_data
description: Retrieve trends in DNS queries to the 1.1.1.1 resolver.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of DNS data to retrieve.

normalization.description: Normalization method applied to results. timeseries_groups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX.

name: get_l7_attack_data
description: Retrieve application layer (L7) attack trends.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

normalization.description: Normalization method applied to results. timeseries accepts PERCENTAGE_CHANGE or MIN0_MAX; timeseriesGroups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX; top/attacks accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN_MAX.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. description: Retrieve Email Security trends.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of Email Security data to retrieve.

name: get_internet_speed_data
description: Retrieve summary of bandwidth, latency, jitter, and packet loss, from the previous 90 days of Cloudflare Speed Test.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of Internet speed data to retrieve.

orderBy.description: Specifies the metric to order the results by. Only allowed for top locations and ASes.

name: get_internet_quality_data
description: Retrieves a summary or time series of bandwidth, latency, or DNS response time percentiles from the Radar Internet Quality Index (IQI).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

metric.description: Specifies which metric to return (bandwidth, latency, or DNS response time).

name: get_ai_data
description: Retrieves AI-related data, including traffic from AI user agents, as well as popular models and model tasks specifically from Cloudflare Workers AI.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of AI data to retrieve.

normalization.description: Normalization method applied to results. bots/timeseries_groups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX.

name: get_bgp_hijacks
description: Retrieve BGP hijack events. BGP hijacks occur when an AS announces routes it does not own, potentially redirecting traffic.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

hijackerAsn.description: Filter by the potential hijacker AS of a BGP hijack event.

victimAsn.description: Filter by the potential victim AS of a BGP hijack event.

involvedAsn.description: Filter by ASN involved (as hijacker or victim) in a BGP event.

involvedCountry.description: Filter by country code of the involved AS in a BGP event.

minConfidence.description: Filter by minimum confidence score (1-4 low, 5-7 mid, 8+ high).

maxConfidence.description: Filter by maximum confidence score (1-4 low, 5-7 mid, 8+ high).

name: get_bgp_leaks
description: Retrieve BGP route leak events. Route leaks occur when an AS improperly announces routes learned from one peer to another.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

leakAsn.description: Filter by the leaking AS of a route leak event.

involvedAsn.description: Filter by ASN involved (as hijacker or victim) in a BGP event.

involvedCountry.description: Filter by country code of the involved AS in a BGP event.

name: get_bgp_route_stats
description: Retrieve BGP routing table statistics including number of routes, origin ASes, and more.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

name: get_bots_data
description: Retrieve bot traffic data including trends by bot name, operator, category, and kind. Covers AI crawlers, search engines, monitoring bots, and more.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

botOperator.description: Filter results by bot operator (e.g., Google, Microsoft, OpenAI).

botKind.description: Filter results by bot kind (AGENT or BOT).

botVerificationStatus.description: Filter results by bot verification status.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of bot data to retrieve.

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

name: get_certificate_transparency_data
description: Retrieve Certificate Transparency (CT) log data. CT provides visibility into SSL/TLS certificates issued for domains, useful for security monitoring.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

caOwner.description: Filter results by certificate authority owner.

entryType.description: Filter results by entry type (certificate vs. pre-certificate).

validationLevel.description: Filter results by validation level (DV, OV, EV).

publicKeyAlgorithm.description: Filter results by public key algorithm.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of Certificate Transparency data to retrieve.

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

normalization.description: Normalization method for CT data. timeseries_groups and summary accept RAW_VALUES or PERCENTAGE.

name: get_netflows_data
description: Retrieve NetFlows traffic data showing network traffic patterns. Supports filtering by ADM1 (administrative level 1, e.g., states/provinces) via geoId.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

geoId.description: Filters results by Geolocation (ADM1 - administrative level 1, e.g., states/provinces). Provide an array of GeoNames IDs. Prefix with '-' to exclude.

product.description: Filter results by network traffic product type.

normalization.description: Normalization method. timeseries accepts PERCENTAGE_CHANGE or MIN0_MAX; timeseries_groups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of NetFlows data to retrieve.

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

name: list_origins
description: List cloud provider origins (hyperscalers) available in Cloud Observatory. Returns Amazon (AWS), Google (GCP), Microsoft (Azure), and Oracle (OCI) with their available regions.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

name: get_origin_details
description: Get details for a specific cloud provider origin, including all available regions.

slug.description: The cloud provider origin to query. Supported values: AMAZON (AWS), GOOGLE (GCP), MICROSOFT (Azure), ORACLE (OCI).

name: get_origins_data
description: Retrieve cloud provider (AWS, GCP, Azure, OCI) performance metrics. Supports timeseries, summaries grouped by region/success_rate/percentile, and grouped timeseries.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of origins data to retrieve. timeseries: Raw time series data (requires metric parameter). summary/*: Aggregated data grouped by dimension. timeseriesGroups/*: Time series grouped by dimension.

origin.description: Array of cloud provider origins to query. At least one origin must be specified.

metric.description: The performance metric to retrieve. CONNECTION_FAILURES: Number of failed connections. REQUESTS: Total request count. RESPONSE_HEADER_RECEIVE_DURATION: Time to receive response headers (ms). TCP_HANDSHAKE_DURATION: TCP handshake time (ms). TCP_RTT: TCP round-trip time (ms). TLS_HANDSHAKE_DURATION: TLS handshake time (ms).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

region.description: Filters results by cloud provider region. Example regions: us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1.

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

normalization.description: Normalization method. timeseries_groups accepts PERCENTAGE or MIN0_MAX.

name: get_robots_txt_data
description: Retrieve robots.txt analysis data. Shows how websites configure crawler access rules, particularly for AI crawlers. Useful for understanding web crawler policies across domains.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

directive.description: Filter by robots.txt directive type (ALLOW or DISALLOW).

pattern.description: Filter by pattern matching type (FULLY or PARTIALLY matched).

userAgentCategory.description: Filter by user agent category (currently only AI is supported).

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of robots.txt data to retrieve.

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: get_bots_crawlers_data
description: Retrieve web crawler HTTP request data. Shows crawler traffic patterns by client type, user agent, referrer, and industry. Useful for analyzing crawler behavior and traffic distribution.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

dimension.description: Dimension for crawler data. CLIENT_TYPE: crawler type, USER_AGENT: crawler user agent, REFERER: referrer analysis, CRAWL_REFER_RATIO: crawl to referrer ratio, VERTICAL: industry vertical, INDUSTRY: industry classification.

format.description: Format for crawler data: summary or time series grouped data.

botOperator.description: Filter results by bot operator (e.g., Google, Microsoft, OpenAI).

limitPerGroup.description: Limits the number of items per group. Extra items appear grouped under 'other'.

name: list_bots
description: List known bots with their details. Includes AI crawlers, search engines, monitoring bots, and more. Filter by category, operator, kind, or verification status.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

name: get_bot_details
description: Get detailed information about a specific bot by its slug identifier.

name: get_leaked_credentials_data
description: Retrieve trends in HTTP authentication requests and compromised credential detection. Shows distribution by compromised status and bot class.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of leaked credentials data to retrieve.

name: get_as112_data
description: Retrieve AS112 DNS sink hole data. AS112 handles reverse DNS lookups for private IP addresses (RFC 1918). Useful for analyzing DNS misconfiguration patterns.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Dimension indicating the type and format of AS112 data to retrieve. AS112 is a DNS sink hole for reverse DNS lookups of private IP addresses.

name: list_geolocations
description: List available geolocations (ADM1 - administrative divisions like states/provinces). Use this to find GeoNames IDs for filtering HTTP and NetFlows data by region.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

name: get_geolocation_details
description: Get details for a specific geolocation by its GeoNames ID.

geoId.description: GeoNames ID for the geolocation (e.g., '2267056' for Lisbon).

name: get_tcp_resets_timeouts_data
description: Retrieve TCP connection quality metrics including resets and timeouts. Useful for understanding connection reliability across networks and locations.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

dimension.description: Format for TCP resets/timeouts data: summary or time series grouped data.

name: get_annotations
description: Retrieve annotations including Internet events, outages, and anomalies from various Cloudflare data sources.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

name: get_outages
description: Retrieve Internet outages and anomalies. Provides information about detected connectivity issues across ASes and locations.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

name: list_ct_authorities
description: List Certificate Authorities (CAs) tracked in Certificate Transparency logs.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

name: get_ct_authority_details
description: Get details for a specific Certificate Authority by its SHA256 fingerprint.

caSlug.description: The Certificate Authority SHA256 fingerprint (64 hexadecimal characters).

name: list_ct_logs
description: List Certificate Transparency logs.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

name: get_ct_log_details
description: Get details for a specific Certificate Transparency log by its slug.

logSlug.description: The Certificate Transparency log slug identifier.

name: get_bgp_timeseries
description: Retrieve BGP updates time series data. Shows BGP announcement and withdrawal patterns over time.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

updateType.description: Filter by BGP update type (ANNOUNCEMENT or WITHDRAWAL).

name: get_bgp_top_ases
description: Get top Autonomous Systems by BGP update count.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

updateType.description: Filter by BGP update type (ANNOUNCEMENT or WITHDRAWAL).

name: get_bgp_top_prefixes
description: Get top IP prefixes by BGP update count.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

updateType.description: Filter by BGP update type (ANNOUNCEMENT or WITHDRAWAL).

name: get_bgp_moas
description: Get Multi-Origin AS (MOAS) prefixes. MOAS occurs when a prefix is announced by multiple ASes, which can indicate hijacking or legitimate anycast.

name: get_bgp_pfx2as
description: Get prefix-to-ASN mapping. Useful for looking up which AS announces a given IP prefix.

longestPrefixMatch.description: Whether to use longest prefix match.

name: get_bgp_ip_space_timeseries
description: Retrieve announced IP address space time series data. Shows the count of announced IPv4 /24s and IPv6 /48s over time. Essential for monitoring BGP route withdrawals, IPv6 address space changes, and detecting significant routing events by ASN or country.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

ipVersion.description: Filters results by IP version (IPv4 vs. IPv6). Useful for monitoring IPv6 address space specifically.

name: get_bgp_routes_realtime
description: Get real-time BGP routes for a specific IP prefix using public route collectors (RouteViews and RIPE RIS). Shows current routing state including AS paths, RPKI validation status, and visibility across peers. Useful for troubleshooting routing issues and verifying route announcements.

name: get_as_set
description: Get IRR AS-SETs that an Autonomous System is a member of. AS-SETs are used in routing policies.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

name: get_as_relationships
description: Get AS-level relationships for an Autonomous System. Shows peer, upstream, and downstream relationships with other ASes.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN), must be a positive number.

asn2.description: Optional second ASN to check specific relationship.

name: list_tlds
description: List top-level domains (TLDs) including generic, country-code, and sponsored TLDs. Filter by type or manager.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

manager.description: Filters results by TLD manager (e.g., 'VeriSign Global Registry Services').

tld.description: Filters results by top-level domain. Specify a comma-separated list of TLDs.

name: get_tld_details
description: Get detailed information about a specific top-level domain (TLD).

name: get_domains_ranking_timeseries
description: Get domain ranking timeseries data. Track how specific domains rank over time.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

domains.description: Filters results by domain name. Specify an array of domain names to track.

domainCategory.description: Filters results by domain category (e.g., 'News & Media', 'Technology').

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: get_speed_histogram
description: Get speed test histogram data. Shows distribution of speed test results for bandwidth, latency, or jitter.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

asn.description: Filters results by ASN. Provide an array of ASN strings. Prefix with '-' to exclude (e.g., ['-174', '3356'] excludes AS174 and includes AS3356). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

continent.description: Filters results by continent. Provide an array of alpha-2 continent codes (e.g., 'EU', 'NA'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values, each array element maps positionally to each dateRange.

location.description: Filters results by location. Provide an array of alpha-2 country codes (e.g., 'US', 'PT'). Prefix a code with '-' to exclude it (e.g., ['-US', 'PT'] excludes the US and includes Portugal). IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values (e.g., ['7d', '7dcontrol']), each array element maps positionally to each dateRange. To compare the same location across time periods, repeat the location code.

metric.description: Metrics to be returned in the histogram. Defaults to BANDWIDTH.

bucketSize.description: Specifies the width for every bucket in the histogram.

name: get_internet_services_timeseries
description: Track internet service ranking changes over time. Useful for monitoring how services like ChatGPT, Google, etc. rank over time.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. IMPORTANT: When using multiple dateRange values for comparison, filter array parameters (location, asn, continent, geoId) map positionally to each dateRange element.

dateStart.description: Start of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateEnd or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End of the date range. Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

serviceCategory.description: Filters results by Internet service category.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: get_outages_by_location
description: Get outage counts aggregated by location. Useful for identifying which countries have the most Internet outages.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

name: get_traffic_anomalies_by_location
description: Get traffic anomalies aggregated by location. Shows which countries have the most detected outage signals, automatically detected by Radar.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

name: get_bgp_routing_table_ases
description: List all ASes in global routing tables with routing statistics (prefix counts, IPv4/IPv6 address count, RPKI validation status). Data comes from public BGP MRT archives.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

location.description: Filters results by location. Specify a valid alpha-2 location code.

sortBy.description: Sort ASes by: cone (customer cone size), pfxs (total prefixes), ipv4/ipv6 (address count), or rpki_* (RPKI validation status).

name: get_bgp_top_ases_by_prefixes
description: Get top ASes ordered by announced prefix count. Useful for understanding which networks have the largest routing footprint. Data comes from public BGP MRT archives and updates every 2 hours.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: get_bgp_rpki_aspa_snapshot
description: Retrieve a snapshot of current or historical RPKI ASPA (Autonomous System Provider Authorization) objects. ASPA objects define which ASNs are authorized upstream providers for a customer ASN, helping prevent route leaks and hijacks.

customerAsn.description: Filter by customer ASN (the ASN that authorizes upstream providers).

providerAsn.description: Filter by provider ASN (the authorized upstream provider ASN).

location.description: Filter by country (alpha-2 code).

date.description: Date for historical ASPA snapshot (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2024-01-15).

page.description: Page number for paginated ASPA results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page for ASPA queries (1-100).

sortBy.description: Sort ASPA results by specified field.

name: get_bgp_rpki_aspa_changes
description: Retrieve RPKI ASPA changes over time, including additions, removals, and modifications of ASPA objects.

customerAsn.description: Filter by customer ASN (the ASN that authorizes upstream providers).

providerAsn.description: Filter by provider ASN (the authorized upstream provider ASN).

changeType.description: Filter by type of ASPA change.

location.description: Filter by country (alpha-2 code).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

sortBy.description: Sort ASPA results by specified field.

page.description: Page number for paginated ASPA results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page for ASPA queries (1-100).

name: get_bgp_rpki_aspa_timeseries
description: Retrieve a timeseries of RPKI ASPA object counts over time.

location.description: Filter by country (alpha-2 code).

dateRange.description: Filters results by date range. For example, use '7d' and '7dcontrol' to compare this week with the previous week. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (dateStart and dateEnd parameters).

dateStart.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-01T00:00:00Z). Either use this parameter together with dateEnd, or use dateRange.

dateEnd.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2023-04-30T23:59:59Z). Either use this parameter together with dateStart, or use dateRange.

name: search_url_scans
description: Search URL scans using ElasticSearch-like query syntax. Examples: 'page.domain:example.com', 'verdicts.malicious:true', 'page.asn:AS24940 AND hash:xxx', 'apikey:me AND date:[2025-01 TO 2025-02]'

query.description: ElasticSearch-like query to filter scans. Examples: 'page.domain:example.com', 'verdicts.malicious:true', 'page.asn:AS24940', 'date:[2025-01 TO 2025-02]'

size.description: Limit the number of results (1-100, default 10).

name: create_url_scan
description: Submit a URL to scan. Returns the scan UUID which can be used to retrieve results.

visibility.description: Scan visibility. Public scans appear in search results. Unlisted scans require the scan ID to access.

name: get_url_scan
description: Get the results of a URL scan by its UUID. Returns detailed information including verdicts, page info, requests, cookies, and more.

name: get_url_scan_screenshot
description: Get the screenshot URL for a completed scan.

name: get_url_scan_har
description: Get the HAR (HTTP Archive) data for a completed scan. Contains detailed network request/response information.
